category_title: Children who hardly speak or do not speak at all
scenario_title: Expressing complex concepts during toy-based therapy
minimal_context: The student and therapist are looking at toys during a picture-naming exercise to describe what is happening.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Look at this picture of the family with the toys. Tell me what is happening here.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Ja. (Uses a deictic gesture to point at another toy in the picture)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you tell me more about that?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Nee. (Makes an iconic hand movement without speech, relying on a one-word sentence because they cannot verbally express the complex concept they have in mind)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Look at this picture of the family with the toys. Tell me what is happening here.

STUDENT: Papa.

TUTOR: Okay, what else?

STUDENT: Ja. (Uses a deictic gesture to point at another toy in the picture)

TUTOR: Can you tell me more about that?

STUDENT: Nee. (Makes an iconic hand movement without speech, relying on a one-word sentence because they cannot verbally express the complex concept they have in mind)

task.content: Communicating messages and expressing concepts that were already present cognitively, but which they could not yet express verbally
task.learning_goal: Insert more complex elements into his or her message

context.materials: Toys present in the therapy room, picture-naming exercises
context.time_context: During baseline recordings and subsequent sessions

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When attempting to communicate with their conversational partner while having deficits in their verbal skills
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Relied on the little verbal skills they had to communicate with their conversational partner by using one-word-sentences
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Concepts that were already present cognitively, but which they could not yet express verbally

behavior_patterns.condition: Throughout the study, despite limited verbal expression skills
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Exhibited a strong preference to communicate verbally
